title: Analytical Comment Script
subtitle: Previous Day Market Summary, S/R Lines, Volume Analysis and Direction Prediction
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)

relationship_to_part_1.description: Directly builds on Part 1. Part 1 gave us the visual ghost overlay. Part 2 adds the textual analytical layer — a data table and written commentary sitting beside that visual. Together they form the foundation of the daily briefing concept.
relationship_to_part_1.extends: The S/R lines in Part 2 (prev day H/L as horizontal lines) are the exact same lines Part 1 draws with ShowHighLow=true. Part 2 just adds colour coding (green = support, red = resistance) and labels them explicitly.

depends_on.note: S/R line drawing is identical to Part 1 ShowHighLow=true. Data fetching pattern is the same. Part 2 is an extension not a replacement.

data_requirements.name: current_day_volume
data_requirements.note: Tool performs best as day progresses — volume reading is partial until session closes

table_text_color.description: Color of text in the on-chart data table

table_x_offset.description: Horizontal pixel offset of the table from the chart edge

table_y_offset.description: Vertical pixel offset of the table from the chart top

support_color.description: Color of the support horizontal line (prev day Low)

resistance_color.description: Color of the resistance horizontal line (prev day High)

steps.name: fetch_prev_day_data
steps.description: Fetch all OHLCV fields for yesterday's D1 bar

steps.name: fetch_current_day_volume
steps.description: Fetch today's D1 volume (partial — grows throughout the day)

steps.name: set_support_resistance
steps.description: Set support = prev_day_low, resistance = prev_day_high. Draw coloured horizontal lines.
steps.note: Same as Part 1 ShowHighLow=true but with explicit green/red colour coding

steps.name: classify_market_nature
steps.description: Compare prev_day_close vs prev_day_open to determine session direction

steps.name: volume_sentiment_analysis
steps.description: Compare current day volume vs previous day volume to infer participation trend

logic.higher: current > prev → Bullish sentiment may continue
logic.lower: current < prev → Bearish sentiment may follow

steps.caveat: Volume comparison is time-of-day dependent. At 9am, today's volume will always be lower than yesterday's full-day volume. Normalise by elapsed session percentage for accuracy.

steps.name: generate_market_commentary
steps.description: Produce a plain-English sentence describing the market based on market_nature

steps.name: render_data_table
steps.description: Format all outputs into a structured text panel displayed on the chart

volume_signal_flaw.description: Comparing raw current-day volume to full previous-day volume is misleading early in the session
volume_signal_flaw.fix: Normalise: volume_pace = current_day_volume / (elapsed_minutes / total_session_minutes). Compare volume_pace to prev_day_volume.

direction_signal_simplicity.description: Bullish/Bearish based only on one day's open vs close is a very weak signal. A single large candle can flip the reading incorrectly.
direction_signal_simplicity.fix: Consider multi-day trend context or combine with volume pace to weight the signal.

no_magnitude_consideration.description: A 1-pip bullish close and a 200-pip bullish close both produce 'Bullish'. Range magnitude is ignored.
no_magnitude_consideration.fix: Add ATR-relative range classification: small / normal / large move.

components_to_build.name: BrokerDataAdapter
components_to_build.note: Already defined in Part 1. Must add volume field to get_ohlc response.

components_to_build.name: AnalyticsEngine
components_to_build.note: Pure functions, no side effects. Easy to unit test.

components_to_build.name: DataTableRenderer
components_to_build.note: Renders a key-value data panel on the chart at configurable position. Used by many future parts.

components_to_build.name: ChartRenderer
components_to_build.note: Already defined in Part 1. Part 2 uses draw_hline with colour and label.

keep: The 7-field data table concept — OHLCV + support + resistance in one panel
Bullish/Bearish/Neutral classification of prev day — simple and useful
Colour-coded S/R lines (green=support, red=resistance) — intuitive convention
Volume comparison logic structure — the idea is correct even if raw implementation is flawed
Plain-English commentary generation — good for dashboards, alerts, and logging
discard: Raw volume comparison without time-normalisation — misleading early in session
MQL5 Comment() function — replaced by our DataTableRenderer
ObjectCreate OBJ_HLINE calls — replaced by ChartRenderer.draw_hline
iBarShift, iVolume, iHigh, iLow, iOpen, iClose MQL5 API calls
Trade.mqh include — imported but never used in this script (author oversight)
improve: Time-normalise volume: compare volume_pace not raw volume
Add range magnitude: ATR-relative classification of yesterday's candle size
Add price distance: how far is current price from support/resistance in pips and ATR
Make commentary dynamic: update as price moves, not just on script run
Add multi-day context: is today's open above/below last 3 days' average?
Merge with Part 1: the S/R lines are duplicated — one unified DailyContext module should serve both

duplicated_logic: Fetching prev_day_high and prev_day_low from D1 bar
Drawing horizontal lines at those levels
part_1_overlap.recommendation: Create a shared DailyContextProvider that both Part 1 and Part 2 call. It returns {open, high, low, close, volume, support, resistance} and is the single source of truth for all daily-level data across the toolkit.

article_figures.title: Essential Levels
article_figures.insight: Chart with green support and red resistance horizontal lines drawn at prev day Low and High. Shows price reacting to both levels — this is the visual proof that the levels are meaningful.

article_figures.title: Quick Chart Reference (Data Table)
article_figures.insight: The on-chart Comment() text panel. Shows the table format: Open, Close, High, Low, Volume fields listed vertically. Simple but immediately useful as a daily briefing panel.

article_figures.title: Tool Test Result 1 (Boom 300 Index)
article_figures.insight: Static screenshot showing bearish reading confirmed by price action.

article_figures.title: Setting Parameters (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Shows the 5 input parameter dialog. Confirms colours and offsets are user-configurable.

article_figures.title: Tool Test Result 2 - USDSEK (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Bullish reading. Initial slight bearish move followed by strong bullish impulse — tool prediction aligned.

article_figures.title: Tool Test Result 3 - Crash 900 Index (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Bearish reading confirmed. Swift bearish move with no reversal — tool called it correctly.

article_figures.title: Tool Test Result 4 - USDCNH (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Bullish reading. Market respected support and resistance levels and moved upward as predicted.
